title: Classify prompt lacks conversation history — monitors produce false positives on multi-turn backreferences
body: The classify prompt receives user_text and assistant_text from the current turn only. When a user message backreferences prior turns ('reoutput my original task', 'as I said earlier'), the classifier cannot verify whether the response addresses the referenced content. It sees a short user request and a long technical response, pattern-matches to 'deviation', and flags incorrectly. Observed: 3 false positives in a single session from the hedge monitor, all on responses that directly addressed what the user asked. Root cause: no conversation_history collector exists. The classifier reasons in a single-turn vacuum. Fix: add a conversation_history context collector that provides the last N turns, or expand user_text to include referenced prior messages.
resolved_by: conversation_history collector with adaptive window — regex-based referential detection, 1-3 turn summaries, 2000 char budget
